summary: The raw strain correlation score has a mean of 83.5144 for all links. The raw strain correlation score has a mean of 14.667 for validated links. Standardising the strain correlation score gives a mean of -0.006 for all links. Standardising

IOKR top-1 accuracy on MIBiG data is 0.1208. IOKR top-5 accuracy on MIBiG data is 0.1708. IOKR top-10 accuracy on MIBiG data is 0.1870. IOKR top-20 accuracy on MIBiG data is 0.2121. IOKR top-200 accuracy on MIBiG data is 0.2946. IOKR AUC on
IOKR mean score for all links is 0.0105.
IOKR mean score for validated links is 0.0364.
The standardised strain correlation score is significantly enriched for validated links at the 90th percentile with a p-value of 2.483 × 10−11.
The IOKR score is significantly enriched for validated links at the 90th percentile with a p-value of 0.0139.
Links scoring above the 90th percentile on both IOKR and standardised strain correlation scores are significantly enriched for validated links with a p-value of 2.633 × 10−4.
The combined scoring function assigns the best rank to the validated link in 10 out of 15 cases tested.
The Crüsemann dataset consists of 120 microbial strains with 8 validated links between a BGC and a MF. The Gross dataset consists of 7 strains with 9 validated links between a BGC and a MF.
The Leão dataset contains 4 strains with 5 validated links between a BGC and a specific MS2 spectrum.
The standardised strain correlation score has mean 0 and variance 1.
Standardised scores make comparison between links involving different sizes of GCFs and MFs possible.
The proportion of validated links among all links in the Crüsemann dataset is enriched above the 90th percentile for the standardised correlation score.
The standardised IOKR score is defined by subtracting the expected value from the IOKR score and dividing by its variance.

The combined scoring function ℓ1/2 ranks validated link for BGC0001228 (retimycin A) at number 253. The combined scoring function ℓ1/2 ranks validated link for BGC0000241 (lomaiviticin A) at number 5.
In the case of BGC0000241 (lomaiviticin A), neither scoring function alone assigns an equal or higher score than the correct link.
Standardisation of the strain correlation score improves its effectiveness at prioritising validated links relative to all links.
Combining strain correlation and IOKR scores increases the ratio of validated links among high-scoring links.
Strain correlation and IOKR scores are complementary methods for linking BGCs to metabolites.
